text: Extensive research provides evidence that AchE inhibition is associated with symptoms that are known to be mediated by increased cholinergic signalling. The interaction between increased acetylcholine and enhanced signalling via nicotinic and muscarinic receptors is well-established. Further, cholinergic neurons are known to innervate multiple physiological sites (reviewed in Costa, In Casarett and Doull's Toxicology, Lodish).
